-- 3 of 21 --

THE LEAD
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 21 --

LEAD OBJECTIVE:
• Set the emotional hook
• Think of this as the elevator pitch for your
campaign.
• Tease the most exciting benefits of the
mechanism.
• “Wow, this sounds amazing! Now prove to me
this works, and will work for me.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 21 --

BIG 4 FEELINGS/BENEFITS
• 	Credit: Kyle Milligan, Agora Companies Copy Chief
• New/Only
• Easy/Anybody
• Safe/Predictable
• Big/Fast
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 21 --

Repetition is good;
being 	repetitious 	is not!
Continue to drive home
the main promise… the main idea…
over and over and over.
“If 	you 	have 	an 	important 	point 	to 	make, 	don't 	try 	to 	be
subtle 	or 	clever. 	Use 	a 	pile 	driver. 	Hit 	the 	point 	once.
Then 	come 	back 	and 	hit 	it 	again. 	Then 	hit 	it 	a 	third 	time
- 	a 	tremendous 	whack.” 	- 	Winston 	Churchill
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 21 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 21 --

2 LEAD QUICKIES:
• Story Leads can be killer! But, they are usually
either boom or bust! Beware.
• The prospect must be able to see themselves in
the story. And done correctly, it’s not just a pattern
interrupt.
• Problem - Solution Leads: Hit the problem, agitate
the problem, then get to the solution as early as
you can. The hope comes from the solution.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 21 --

MARKETING
ARGUMENT
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 21 --

Everything I do, say, show, demonstrate in
the body copy (Marketing Argument) is
there for one reason and one reason only…
ESTABLISH THE BUYING BELIEF!
(Campaign Thesis)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 21 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 21 --

PRESENTING
PROOF
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 21 --

Don’t Claim
What You Can’t Prove
No Claim Should Be Bigger Than Your Biggest Proof Point!
You Must Give 	Support/Proof
For Whatever You Claim
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 21 --

CLAIM PROOF 	SOCIAL PROOF
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 21 --

Presenting “Social Proof”
Present testimonials and case
studies strategically throughout
offer copy to serve as proof for
product outcome claims.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 21 --

Presenting “Claim Proof”
• The reason why is…
• Because…
• “This ensures your ad account never gets banned
because it leverages only compliant copy.”
• “Your grass will grow rapidly. The reason why is
because of the fast uptake of nutrients which
trigger growth chemicals in the seed.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 21 --

The Proof Power Of 3
• One is an event…
• Two is a coincidence…
• Three is a pattern.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 21 --

The Proof Power Of 3
• For big, bold claims…
• Support it with three consecutive paragraphs of proof.
• Put your strongest proof at the top, and devote the
most ink to it. The next paragraph should be about
half the length of the first. And the third should be
half the length of the second.
• You should vary, if you can, the type of proof you
provide.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 21 --

In a test conducted by Buyer Trends last year, Skippy was rated as
the top-scoring peanut butter in every single category: aroma,
appearance, texture, and taste. When compared to other popular
brands, such as Jif and Peter Pan, it rated between two and five
points higher in each of these categories. Jeff Goodman, reporting
for Buyer Trends, said, "When it comes to the pleasure of eating,
Skippy rules. "
A scientific analysis conducted by WebNutrition.com found that
Skippy had the highest ratio of protein to fat of all the major
brands. Patrick Dunney, president of the National Health Institute,
said that Skippy was "far and away " the best product from a health
point of view.
I used to be a Jif fan myself. But last Thursday, for the first time, I
tried Skippy on a slice of whole wheat toast—and I was blown away
by how good it is!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 21 --

During Your Research Keep
Your Eyes Peeled For
Monster Proof Points
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 21 --
